scope_summary.problem: mb_* runtime functions have mixed return ownership semantics (new vs borrowed refs). JIT releases all locals at return, causing use-after-free for borrowed refs → requirements.md, issue #1129 'Blocked: Ownership Audit'
scope_summary.success_criteria: All mb_* functions return owned references (new ref or retained borrowed ref). EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS=true with no ASan violations. GC re-enabled → pre_clarifications Q1 (exhaustive audit), Q2 (include GC)

boundary.in_scope: Runtime ownership audit (all mb_* functions), enable EMIT_REFCOUNT_CALLS, re-enable GC → spec_plan: jit-refcount-audit, gc-reenable
boundary.out_of_scope: New JIT codegen features, container optimizations, cycle collector improvements
boundary.constraints: Must maintain CPython 3.12 semantics (new ref = caller owns, borrowed ref = must retain). Immortal objects must never be freed.
